author: INCiTE Web
attribution_text: UNIK electronic GPS roadbook by INCiTE Web, via Wikimedia Commons, licensed CC BY-SA 4.0 (https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by-sa/4.0)
description: Modern UNIK electronic GPS roadbook unit ('GPS Roadbook electronique'). Head-on screen view. Low resolution (377x599) but the only true modern digital roadbook device found under a CC license.

author: San Andreas
attribution_text: Honda XRV 750 Africa Twin electronic tripmaster, photo by San Andreas, via Wikimedia Commons, licensed CC BY-SA 3.0 (https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by-sa/3.0)
description: Electronic 'Tripmaster' odometer/computer mounted on a Honda XRV 750 Africa Twin rally motorcycle. Close, fairly head-on shot of the device. High resolution (3504x2336). Best fit for an MX Bikes handlebar device.

author: _morgado
attribution_text: Terratrip rally tripmaster on a rally car dashboard (Castelo Branco Classic Auto), photo by _morgado, via Wikimedia Commons, licensed CC BY 2.0 (https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by/2.0)
description: Terratrip rally tripmaster computer mounted on a classic rally car dashboard. Portrait crop showing the unit fairly head-on. Moderate resolution (828x1242).

author: _morgado
attribution_text: Terratrip rally tripmaster (Rali de Castelo Branco 2017), photo by _morgado, via Wikimedia Commons, licensed CC BY 2.0 (https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by/2.0)
description: Terratrip-equipped rally car cockpit, Rali de Castelo Branco 2017. Very high resolution (7360x4912). Shows tripmaster/odometer in the dash context; will need cropping to isolate the device.

author: _morgado
attribution_text: Terratrip rally tripmaster (Rali de Castelo Branco 2017), photo by _morgado, via Wikimedia Commons, licensed CC BY 2.0 (https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by/2.0)
description: Companion frame to DSC_5366: Terratrip-equipped rally car cockpit, Rali de Castelo Branco 2017. Very high resolution (7360x4912). Needs cropping to isolate the tripmaster.

author: Morio
attribution_text: Hino Team Sugawara Dakar roadbook holders (Tokyo Motor Show 2013), photo by Morio, via Wikimedia Commons, licensed CC BY-SA 3.0 (https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by-sa/3.0)
description: Dakar-spec motorized roadbook holders (paper scroll boxes with wind knobs) on the Hino Team Sugawara truck dashboard, Tokyo Motor Show 2013. High resolution (3840x2560). Shows the classic scroll-box reader clearly.

author: Morio
attribution_text: Hino Team Sugawara Dakar roadbook holder close-up (Tokyo Motor Show 2013), photo by Morio, via Wikimedia Commons, licensed CC BY-SA 3.0 (https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by-sa/3.0)
description: Closer square-format view of a Hino Team Sugawara motorized roadbook scroll holder, Tokyo Motor Show 2013. Resolution 2560x2560. Good reference for the scroll window + wind knobs.

author: Ludovic Peron
attribution_text: Roadbook mounted on a handlebar stem ('roadbook sur potence'), Criterium du Dauphine 2014, photo by Ludovic Peron, via Wikimedia Commons, licensed CC BY-SA 3.0 (https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by-sa/3.0)
description: Paper roadbook clipped to a handlebar-stem holder ('sur potence') on a bicycle, Criterium du Dauphine 2014. Downloaded at 3840x5760 via Wikimedia Special:FilePath (original is 4032x6048). Note: cycling rather than rally-raid, but the stem-mounted holder form factor is a useful reference.

author: Thomas Springer (Ordercrazy)
attribution_text: Winmate rugged computing tablet PC with edge protection (Embedded World 2014), photo by Thomas Springer (Ordercrazy), via Wikimedia Commons, dedicated to the public domain under CC0 1.0 (https://creativecommons.org/publicdomain/zero/1.0/)
description: Winmate fully-rugged tablet with thick black rubber edge bumpers, shot nearly head-on/face-on on a simple orange show-stand background. The screen is a clean flat rectangle facing the camera - ideal for masking out and compositing a roadbook through it. CC0 (no attribution required), so the preferred masking base. Resolution 2603x1949.

author: OutdoorMen
attribution_text: Rugged industrial and construction tablet (x-tab), photo by OutdoorMen, via Wikimedia Commons, licensed CC BY-SA 4.0 (https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by-sa/4.0)
description: Second rugged tablet, shot at a slight three-quarter angle on a plain background. Higher resolution (4592x2576) than the Winmate but more angled, so it needs a small perspective correction before the screen can be masked. Good alternate / hero shot. Requires CC BY-SA attribution.
